Tests the behavior of the modulus operator with `undefined` in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the result of the modulus operation between `undefined` and the number `1`. The expression `undefined % 1` evaluates to `NaN` (Not-a-Number), and the `assert` function verifies that this result is indeed `NaN` using the `isNaN` function. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a test to confirm the behavior of the modulus operator when applied to `undefined`. It is likely part of a larger test suite to ensure correct handling of edge cases in arithmetic operations.
